definition: A saturated hydrocarbon with only single bonds; general formula CnH2n+2
A hydrocarbon with at least one carbon-carbon double bond; general formula CnH2n
A nucleophilic substitution that proceeds through a carbocation intermediate in a two-step
A single-step nucleophilic substitution with backside attack; rate depends on both substrate and nucleophile concentration
The property of a molecule that is not superimposable on its mirror image; typically due to a carbon with four different groups
One of two stereoisomers that are mirror images of each other and cannot be superimposed

column3: the hydrogen attaches to the carbon that already has more hydrogens

definition: Nuclear magnetic resonance — an analytical technique that determines molecular structure by measuring nuclear spin behavior in a magnetic field
